Name: Wild Berries
Description: A handful of tart wild berries plucked from a roadside bush; tart but nourishing.

Name: Dried Mushrooms
Description: A cluster of edible mushrooms dried in the sun; bland but filling.

Name: Wild Roots
Description: Thick starchy roots dug from soft earth; chewy and earthy in flavor.

Name: Foraged Ration
Description: A day's worth of foraged plants and fungi bundled together for travel.

Name: Small Game
Description: A small animal — rabbit or squirrel — field-dressed and ready to cook.

Name: Fresh Water
Description: A flask of clean water gathered from a stream or natural spring.

Name: Pine Nuts
Description: Hard seeds shaken from pine cones; dense and caloric for cold travel.

Name: Edible Tuber
Description: A starchy underground tuber that provides solid sustenance when roasted.

Name: Bitter Greens
Description: Leafy greens pulled from the ground's edge; nutritious despite the sharp flavor.

Name: River Fish
Description: A small fish caught in a shallow river or pool; mild flesh and easy bones.

Name: Wild Mushroom
Description: A single firm mushroom with savory flavor; safe to eat and lightly filling.

Name: Tropical Fruit
Description: A brightly colored jungle fruit with sweet pulp and high sugar content.

Name: Coconut
Description: A green coconut knocked from a palm; the water inside is clean and refreshing.

Name: Cactus Fruit
Description: A spiny fruit cut from a desert cactus; juicy inside with a mildly sweet taste.

Name: Cactus Water
Description: Water painstakingly extracted from a cactus core; enough to sustain one person.

Name: Frozen Berries
Description: Small berries preserved in ice; slightly tart and dense with nutrients.

Name: Snow Hare
Description: A white-furred arctic hare hunted on the tundra; lean but warming meat.

Name: Cave Mushroom
Description: A pale fungus growing in the dark; edible and surprisingly rich in flavor.

Name: Blind Fish
Description: A translucent fish pulled from an underground pool; soft flesh and mild taste.

Name: Mountain Goat Meat
Description: Tough but flavorful meat from a mountain goat; requires slow cooking.

Name: Seaweed
Description: Briny strands of dried seaweed; nutritious and useful as a trail supplement.

Name: Shellfish
Description: A handful of clams or mussels pried from rocks at the shoreline; salty and filling.

Name: Coastal Fish
Description: A larger fish speared or trapped near the coast; firm white flesh.

Name: Seeds and Grains
Description: A pouch of gathered wild seeds; not a meal alone but useful mixed into food.

Name: Acorns
Description: A handful of dried acorns from a forest floor; bitter raw but edible when leached.

Name: Salvaged Foodstuffs
Description: Partially-preserved food scavenged from ruins or abandoned structures.

Name: Common Herb
Description: A broadly useful medicinal herb found in most temperate regions; treats minor ailments.

Name: Healing Moss
Description: Soft absorbent moss with mild antiseptic properties; applied as a wound dressing.

Name: Willow Bark
Description: Strips of bark known to reduce fever and dull pain when brewed into tea.

Name: Alchemist's Weed
Description: A pungent herb used as a base reagent in minor alchemical preparations.

Name: Medicinal Root
Description: A dried root with anti-inflammatory properties; sought by herbalists and healers.

Name: Desert Sage
Description: Aromatic sage from arid regions; used in purification rituals and antiseptic poultices.

Name: Ice Moss
Description: Pale bluish moss that grows near permafrost; prized by cold-climate healers.

Name: Phosphorescent Moss
Description: Softly glowing moss harvested from cave walls; used in light-based alchemical reagents.

Name: Swamp Moss
Description: Thick spongy moss from boggy soil; absorbs toxins and is used in antidote crafting.

Name: Rare Flower
Description: An unusual bloom found in untamed wilderness; gathered for perfume and alchemical use.

Name: Jungle Orchid
Description: An exotic orchid found in tropical undergrowth; highly prized by alchemists and mages.

Name: Mountain Flower
Description: A hardy alpine bloom found above the treeline; used in cold-weather tonics.

Name: Menga Leaves
Description: Large waxy leaves with soporific compounds; the raw material for sleep-inducing draughts.

Name: Alchemical Lichen
Description: A slow-growing crust lichen with rare chemical properties; used in transmutation work.

Name: Scorpion Venom
Description: Carefully extracted venom from a desert scorpion; potent and sought by poisoners.

Name: Leech
Description: A live blood-sucking leech used medicinally to reduce swelling and purge toxins.

Name: Crystal Fragment
Description: A piece of natural crystal broken from a cave formation; mildly resonant with magical energy.

Name: Salt Crystal
Description: A chunk of raw salt from a coastal evaporation pool or mineral seep; useful for preserving food.

Name: Swamp Gas Flask
Description: A sealed flask of captured swamp gas; unstable and flammable if exposed to flame.

Name: Alchemist Discards
Description: Partially-processed alchemical materials left behind in urban waste; still viable as reagents.

Name: Sandstone Chunk
Description: A block of fine-grained sandstone; occasionally contains trace minerals useful in alchemy.

Name: Firewood Bundle
Description: A bundle of dry branches and split wood; enough to sustain a campfire through the night.

Name: Green Wood
Description: Freshly cut limbs from living trees; heavier than dry wood and not yet suitable for burning.

Name: Hardwood Branch
Description: A dense branch from a mature hardwood tree; useful for tool hafts and structural supports.

Name: Driftwood
Description: Smooth weathered wood washed ashore; good for fire or rough carving.

Name: Rope Fiber
Description: Strong plant fibers stripped from stalks or vines; can be twisted into serviceable rope.

Name: Vine Cord
Description: A length of tough jungle vine; flexible

Name: Grass Fiber
Description: Dried long grass braided into cords; fine for light lashing and weaving.

Name: Tree Resin
Description: Thick amber-colored sap collected from conifers; useful as a sealant and fire starter.

Name: Mineral Deposit
Description: A small chunk of ore-bearing rock chipped from a mountain face; contains trace metal content.

Name: Eagle Feather
Description: A large primary feather from a mountain eagle; prized for fletching and ritual use.

Name: Old Coins
Description: A scatter of corroded coins found in rubble; worth melting down or cleaning for value.

Name: Useful Scraps
Description: Assorted salvaged metal and wood bits gathered from ruins; handy for repairs and improvisation.

Name: Bone Shard
Description: A pointed shard of animal bone found near old remains; useful for crude tools or fetishes.
